annotations.label: Complex conjugate

anchor.snippet: the complex conjugate of a complex number is the number with an equal real part and an imaginary part equal in magnitude but opposite in sign

annotations.note: Complex conjugation is the `star` operation of `Complex.instStarRing`; `conj_re` and `conj_im` (in `Mathlib/Data/Complex/Basic.lean`) confirm the same-real-part / negated-imaginary-part definition.

annotations.label: Conjugate in polar form
annotations.note: Polar coordinates for complex numbers are formalized as `Complex.polarCoord`, but there is no dedicated lemma stating `conj (r e^{iخ¸}) = r e^{-iخ¸}` in this form.

annotations.label: Product of a number and its conjugate is real

anchor.snippet: The product of a complex number and its conjugate is a real number

annotations.note: `Complex.mul_conj` states `z * conj z = (normSq z : â„‚)`, exhibiting the product as a (coerced) real number.

annotations.label: Complex conjugate root theorem

anchor.snippet: If a root of a univariate polynomial with real coefficients is complex, then its complex conjugate is also a root

annotations.note: `RCLike.aeval_conj` gives `aeval (conj z) p = conj (aeval z p)` for `p : â„‌[X]`, immediately yielding the conjugate-root theorem (used as `simpa [dvd_iff_isRoot, aeval_conj]` in `Analysis/Complex/Polynomial/Basic.lean`).

annotations.label: Conjugation distributes over arithmetic

anchor.snippet: For any two complex numbers, conjugation is distributive over addition, subtraction, multiplication and division

annotations.note: `starRingEnd â„‚` is a ring homomorphism (a `RingHom`), so `map_add`, `map_sub`, `map_mul`, `map_div` give distributivity over all arithmetic operations.

annotations.label: Fixed points of conjugation are reals

anchor.snippet: A complex number is equal to its complex conjugate if its imaginary part is zero

annotations.note: `Complex.conj_eq_iff_im` states `conj z = z â†” z.im = 0`, with `conj_eq_iff_real` giving the equivalent characterisation via realness.

annotations.label: Conjugation preserves modulus

anchor.snippet: Conjugation does not change the modulus of a complex number

annotations.note: `Complex.norm_conj` (and `RCLike.norm_conj`) state `â€–conj zâ€– = â€–zâ€–`; `Complex.normSq_conj` provides the squared version.

annotations.label: Conjugation is an involution
annotations.note: The `InvolutiveStar` field `star_involutive : Function.Involutive star` applies to `â„‚` via `Complex.instStarRing` (cf. `star_involutive x := by simp only [eta, neg_neg]` at `Data/Complex/Basic.lean:440`).

annotations.label: Product with conjugate equals modulus squared

anchor.snippet: The product of a complex number with its conjugate is equal to the square of the number's modulus

annotations.note: `Complex.mul_conj` yields `z * conj z = (normSq z : â„‚)` where `normSq z = â€–zâ€–آ²` (`Complex.sq_abs` / `Complex.normSq_eq_abs`).

annotations.label: Conjugation commutes with exp, log, integer powers

anchor.snippet: Conjugation is commutative under composition with exponentiation to integer powers, with the exponential function, and with the natural logarithm for nonzero arguments

annotations.note: `Complex.exp_conj` (used in `JacobiTheta`) and `Complex.log_conj` (in `Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Complex/Log.lean:123`, with side hypothesis `x.arg â‰  د€`) give the exp/log cases; integer-power compatibility follows from `map_pow`/`map_zpow` on the ring homomorphism `starRingEnd â„‚`.

annotations.label: Real polynomial roots in conjugate pairs

anchor.snippet: non-real roots of real polynomials occur in complex conjugate pairs

annotations.note: `RCLike.aeval_conj` is the workhorse and is invoked (via `simpa [dvd_iff_isRoot, aeval_conj]`) in `Analysis/Complex/Polynomial/Basic.lean:180` precisely to derive that non-real roots come in conjugate pairs.

annotations.label: Conjugation commutes with real-valued holomorphic functions
annotations.note: I found no Schwarz-reflection-style lemma `conj (f z) = f (conj z)` for holomorphic functions taking reals to reals in Mathlib (searched for `SchwarzReflection`, `reflection_principle`, `conj.*holomorph`).

annotations.label: Conjugation is a field automorphism fixing R
annotations.note: `Complex.conjAe : â„‚ â‰ƒâ‚گ[â„‌] â„‚` realizes conjugation as an â„‌-algebra automorphism, with `Complex.real_algHom_eq_id_or_conj` showing it is (with the identity) the only one; `Complex.conjCLE`/`conjCAE` give the homeomorphism upgrade.

annotations.label: Recovering real/imaginary parts, modulus, argument from conjugate

anchor.snippet: its conjugate is sufficient to reproduce the parts

annotations.note: `Complex.re_eq_add_conj` (`(z.re : â„‚) = (z + conj z)/2`) and `Complex.sub_conj` (`z - conj z = (2 z.im) i`) cover real/imaginary parts; modulus via `mul_conj`, but I found no direct lemma recovering the argument from `z` and `conj z`.

annotations.label: Lines in the plane via conjugate

anchor.snippet: can be used to specify lines in the plane

annotations.note: I found no formalization of lines in â„‚ expressed in the form `\overline c z + c \overline z = k`.

annotations.label: Conjugation on matrices
annotations.note: `Matrix.conjTranspose` (notation `ل´´`) is defined and packaged as a star operation, additive equiv, linear map, and (in the square case) a `StarRingEquiv` to the opposite ring.

annotations.label: Quaternion conjugation

anchor.snippet: One may also define a conjugation for quaternions and split-quaternions

annotations.note: Quaternion conjugation is the `star` of `Quaternion.instStarRing` (line 605, `star_involutive x := by simp [Star.star]`), with companion lemmas `Quaternion.star_mul_self`, `self_mul_star`, etc.

annotations.label: Generalized conjugation is multiplicative with reversal

anchor.snippet: All these generalizations are multiplicative only if the factors are reversed

annotations.note: `StarMul.star_mul : âˆ€ r s, star (r * s) = star s * star r` (line 123) is exactly the antimultiplicative-with-reversal property satisfied by complex, matrix, quaternion star operations.

annotations.label: Complex conjugation / real structure on a vector space

anchor.snippet: There is also an abstract notion of conjugation for vector spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib has `StarModule` capturing conjugate-linear / star-semilinear structure on a module, but no dedicated `RealStructure` type for an antilinear involution making the real-fixed subspace a real form of a complex vector space.
